goals: 1. Complete correct engine — fix all GAPs, all choices policy-driven
2. Full policy control — every rng decision routes through PolicyCallback
3. Train model that plays reasonable 8-10 turn games with itself (no blunders)
4. Push combined WR vs heuristic to >0.50 (current best: v6_iter20=0.458, new project record)
current_task: v7 PPO COMPLETE (80/80 iters). Bench results collected. v8 design ready: launch from v6_iter20 with heuristic-floor=0.30 and trimmed fixtures. US overfit experiment running (PID 874033, iter 21/40).
current_strategy: v7 completed cleanly (fadeout=999 fix worked — no panel collapse) but heuristic-combined WR REGRESSED vs v6_iter20: v7_iter80=0.423 combined, vs v6_iter20=0.458 and v56=0.441. USSR side gained +16pp (0.558→0.722, new all-time high) but US side collapsed −20pp (0.325→0.124, new all-time low). Candidate tournament: v7_iter40=1999 Elo (+99 vs v56) — strongest vs BC cluster. Diagnosis: US over-specialized to self-similar BC+past-self pool. v6_iter20 remains project record on stated heuristic-WR metric. v8 plan: warmstart from v6_iter20, heuristic-floor 0.15→0.30, drop BC-cluster near-duplicates (v20/v44/v54/v55), keep v56+specialists+heuristic. See opus_analysis_20260420_v7_results_next_steps.md.

iter20.note: TRUE BEST — new project record (+1.7pp over v56)

v6_final_results.root_cause: league_fixture_fadeout=50 caused 100% self-play echo chamber from iter 51+. All fixtures (scripted + __heuristic__) dropped from pool. US side (weaker) collapsed fastest.
v6_final_results.running_best_bug: panel-aggregate WR saturates near 0.5 once past-self dominates the panel, hiding heuristic-side regression. Manual checkpoint selection required.

v7_final_results.status: COMPLETE — 80 iters. Fadeout fix worked (no panel collapse). Heuristic-combined REGRESSED vs v6_iter20.

iter40.note: highest league Elo but still below v6_iter20 on heuristic

iter80.note: USSR all-time high; US all-time low; combined < v6_iter20

panel_avg_trajectory.note: monotone up but misleading — panel is 7:1 league-weighted vs heuristic

iter40.note: WINNER — +99 vs v56, strongest vs BC cluster ever

v7_final_results.diagnosis: US side over-specialized to self-similar BC-cluster+past-self pool. USSR generalized well (+16pp vs heuristic) but US lost generalization (-20pp vs heuristic). Same disease as v6, different symptom — fadeout fix prevented running-best bug, but league-style dominant pool produced directional US regression.

next_tasks: Launch v8 PPO from v6_iter20 with heuristic-floor=0.30, trimmed fixtures (v56 + ussr_only_v5 + us_only_v5 + __heuristic__). See opus_analysis_20260420_v7_results_next_steps.md for exact command.
Cross-check v6_iter20 with second-seed bench (60000/60500) to confirm 0.458 is not a lucky seed before anchoring v8 against it.
Monitor US overfit experiment to iter 40. Success: US WR vs heuristic >= 0.40 at 500-game bench (beats us_only_v5=0.360).

[DEFERRED] (v7 legacy) At v7 iter 80: verify no post-iter-50 regression — DONE: panel OK but heuristic-combined regressed.
Parallel/optional: US-specialist capacity test (ppo_us_overfit_v1) — WAIT until v7 iter 20 checkpoint is confirmed healthy.
Low priority: add heuristic-only gate to running-best selection to prevent echo-chamber best-selection bias.
Low priority: implement --league-past-self-cap K=10 flag (~20 lines).
Month-3 priority: Dirichlet noise at MCTS root (CLAUDE.md priority #1 — Phase 0a).

No model has decisively separated from the BC cluster yet — Elo progress needs something beyond v7 (teacher search, multi-head, or dense reward).
